premise: A battlefield healer discovers she can transfer wounds between people — not heal them, move them. The army wants her to move fatal wounds from officers onto prisoners. A dying general offers her a choice: move his wound onto a prisoner and save the campaign, or let him die and watch the army fragment.

characters.name: Sylvie Dunmore
characters.description: Battlefield healer, mid-20s. Discovered her ability three months ago by accident. Farm-raised, direct, hates the war but believes in the people fighting it. Every wound she transfers leaves a phantom ache in her own body that doesn't fade.

characters.name: Corporal Jien
characters.description: Infantry soldier assigned as Sylvie's escort. Sixteen, lied about his age to enlist. Quiet, watchful, has seen too much. The only person who treats Sylvie like a person instead of an asset. Lost his squad at the river crossing.

characters.name: General Voss
characters.description: The dying general. Sixty, legendary tactician, built the campaign on personal loyalty. Not a tyrant — genuinely believes the campaign will save more lives than it costs. His wound is a gut wound, slow and certain. He's had three days to think about the math of one prisoner's life versus ten thousand soldiers.
